---
title: Enriched motifs in homologous and non-homologous UBCs
date: 2023-04-19
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To identify enriched TF-binding motifs in homologous and non-homologous UBCs. Same as analysis from 2023-02-24 except with the analysis for enriched motifs in homologous UBCs.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<human Aldinger software directory>/run_memes.R` and `<utilities>/​get_promoter_seqs.R`.

Minor changes to the `run_memes.R` file to include a command line argument indicating whether the logFC-positive or -negative genes should be used (i.e., which set of differentially expressed genes to run motif enrichment on). Use `--logfc_pos` or `--logfc_neg`.

As input, the `run_memes.R` script takes a table of differentially expressed genes generated from Seurat's `FindMarkers` function. Here, I use the CSV file generated on 2022-02-05 (`/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230205/de_genes.csv`) which contains the differentially expressed genes in homologous vs. non-homologous UBCs. Genes upregulated in homologous UBCs have a logFC > 0 (hence, `--logfc_pos`) and vice versa for non-homologous UBCs (hence, `--logfc_neg`).

1100 bp promoter sequences were used (1000 bp upstream and 100 bp downstream).

### Output

Enriched motifs in the homologous UBCs are found in the `homol_ubc/` subdirectory.

Enriched motifs in the non-homologous UBCs are found in the `nonhomol_ubc/` subdirectory.

Each subdirectory contains the AME output (`.tsv` and `.html` files) as well as a CSV with all the significantly enriched motifs.


## Results

![Top enriched TF-binding motifs in the homologous UBCs.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230419/homol_ubc/ame_heatmap.png){#fig-homol-motifs}

![Top enriched TF-binding motifs in the non-homologous UBCs.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230419/nonhomol_ubc/ame_heatmap.png){#fig-nonhomol-motifs}

### Comparing enriched motifs in homologous and non-homologous UBCs

Load libraries and data into R.

```{r}
#| output: false

library(tidyverse)
library(ggVennDiagram)

# import functions
source("/CBL_scRNAseq/software/utilities/plot_venn_diagrams.R")

# location of CSV files
out_dir <- "/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230419"
hubc_csv <- file.path(out_dir, "/homol_ubc/enriched_motifs.csv")
nhubc_csv <- file.path(out_dir, "/nonhomol_ubc/enriched_motifs.csv")

# load enriched motifs
hubc_motifs <- read_csv(hubc_csv)
nhubc_motifs <- read_csv(nhubc_csv)
```

Determine the number of overlapping TFs in homologous and non-homologous UBCs.

```{r}
# reformat motif IDs
enr_motifs <- map(
  .x = list(homol = hubc_motifs$motif_id, nonhomol = nhubc_motifs$motif_id),
  .f = \(x) str_remove_all(string = x, pattern = "_.*")
)

enr_motifs
```

Enriched TF-binding motifs found only in homologous UBCs.

```{r}
setdiff(enr_motifs$homol, enr_motifs$nonhomol)
```

Enriched TF-binding motifs found only in non-homologous UBCs.

```{r}
setdiff(enr_motifs$nonhomol, enr_motifs$homol)
```

Enriched TF-binding motifs found in both UBC cell types.

```{r}
intersect(enr_motifs$homol, enr_motifs$nonhomol)
```

Venn diagram showing the number of shared and unique TFs.

```{r}
# prep enriched TF motifs for Venn diagram
venn_data <- prep_venn_data(enr_motifs)

plt <- venn_plot(venn_data, label_intersect = FALSE) + 
  theme_void()

ggsave(
  "enr_motifs_venn.png",
  plot = plt,
  path = out_dir,
  width = 5,
  height = 3,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

plt
```
